title: Create Landing Page Route Component
description: Develop a new static landing page as a React component in the file-based routing system.
details: Create a new file `apps/app/routes/landing.tsx`. Implement a React functional component using TypeScript. Structure the page using semantic HTML elements (e.g., <header>, <main>, <section>). Use Tailwind CSS utility classes for layout, spacing, and typography. Reuse existing components from `apps/app/components/` where appropriate to maintain consistency. Ensure the component is self-contained and does not require any backend API calls or dynamic data.
test_strategy: 1. Run the development server and navigate to `/landing` to visually verify the page renders as expected. 2. Check for TypeScript errors and linter warnings. 3. Confirm the component loads without crashing. 4. Optionally, write a simple unit test to ensure the component renders.

title: Ensure Responsive Design and Accessibility
description: Apply responsive design and accessibility best practices to the landing page component.
details: Use Tailwind CSS responsive modifiers (e.g., `sm:`, `md:`, `lg:`) to ensure the landing page looks good on all screen sizes. Use semantic HTML and ARIA attributes where necessary for accessibility. Test with keyboard navigation and screen readers if possible. Ensure color contrast and font sizes meet accessibility standards.
test_strategy: 1. Resize the browser window and verify layout adapts for mobile, tablet, and desktop. 2. Use browser accessibility tools (e.g., Lighthouse, axe) to check for accessibility issues. 3. Test keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility.

title: Add Navigation Link to Landing Page
description: Integrate a link to the new landing page in the main site navigation for discoverability.
details: Edit `apps/app/components/layout.tsx` (or the appropriate navigation component) to add a link to `/landing`. Use existing navigation styles and components to maintain consistency. Ensure the link is visible and accessible from all pages wrapped by the root layout.
test_strategy: 1. Verify the navigation link appears in the site header or menu. 2. Click the link to ensure it routes to `/landing`. 3. Confirm navigation works on all supported devices and screen sizes.

title: Test Route Integration and Page Functionality
description: Verify that the landing page route is correctly integrated and accessible within the application.
details: Start the frontend development server. Navigate to `/landing` via direct URL entry and via the navigation link. Confirm the page renders correctly, matches the intended layout, and does not break other routes. Optionally, add a simple end-to-end test (e.g., with Playwright or Cypress) to automate route accessibility verification.
test_strategy: 1. Manually test navigation to `/landing` and other routes. 2. Run automated tests (if available) to confirm the new route is accessible and does not cause regressions. 3. Check browser console for errors or warnings.
